title: Reconcile stale spec/lichen-native raw-frame/config draft with LCI CoAP contract
description: Discovered during project-LICHEN-py4c.1.2 review. The current app/LCI contract is IPv6 + CoAP over SLIP/BLE/IP per spec/11-lci.md and docs/ble-app-surface-owner.md, while spec/lichen-native is identified as an older CBOR native protocol draft. The draft also defines raw_rx_enable as config key 64 in 10-raw-frame.md but omits it from 04-config.md. Decide whether to retire, clearly mark, or reconcile spec/lichen-native, and if raw diagnostics remain needed, define the CoAP resource/permission model for raw RX/TX arming with tests/docs. No hardware required.
notes: Evidence from project-LICHEN-py4c.1.2 review: docs/ble-app-surface-owner.md lines 82-90 say direct native BLE LCI is SLIP-framed IPv6 over a LICHEN-specific GATT service and is not the older CBOR native protocol draft in spec/lichen-native. spec/11-lci.md section 17 says LCI treats the local connection as another IPv6 interface, uses CoAP everywhere, and exposes /.well-known/core, /config, /config/radio, /config/identity, /status, /status/neighbors, /status/routes, /keys, /mesh via CoAP/Observe. spec/lichen-native/10-raw-frame.md says raw_rx_enable is config key 64, but spec/lichen-native/04-config.md does not list that config key. Until this is reconciled, Python TUI project-LICHEN-py4c.1.2 treats spec/lichen-native as non-authoritative for implementation and leaves raw diagnostics disabled/advanced placeholder only.
